merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/click/core.py b/src/click/core.py
index 666ad68..3e4fa84 100644
--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -228,6 +228,14 @@ class Context:
         value is not set, it defaults to the value from the parent
         context. ``Command.show_default`` overrides this default for the
         specific command.
+    :param validate_nesting: If enabled, closing this context while any
+        child contexts created through :meth:`_make_sub_context` are
+        still open raises a ``RuntimeError``. This helps catch resource
+        leaks caused by improperly nested contexts. Child contexts
+        inherit this setting. The default is ``False``.
+
+    .. versionchanged:: 8.2
+        Added the ``validate_nesting`` parameter.
 
     .. versionchanged:: 8.2
         The ``protected_args`` attribute is deprecated and will be removed in
@@ -280,6 +288,7 @@ class Context:
         token_normalize_func: t.Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
         color: bool | None = None,
         show_default: bool | None = None,
+        validate_nesting: bool = False,
     ) -> None:
         #: the parent context or `None` if none exists.
         self.parent = parent
@@ -432,6 +441,20 @@ class Context:
         self._parameter_source: dict[str, ParameterSource] = {}
         self._exit_stack = ExitStack()
 
+        #: Whether to enforce that child contexts created from this
+        #: context (see :meth:`_make_sub_context`) are closed before
+        #: this context is closed. This helps catch resource leaks
+        #: caused by improperly nested contexts.
+        #:
+        #: .. versionadded:: 8.2
+        self.validate_nesting = validate_nesting
+        #: Open child contexts created from this context, tracked only
+        #: when :attr:`validate_nesting` is enabled.
+        self._children: list[Context] = []
+
+        if parent is not None:
+            parent._children.append(self)
+
     @property
     def protected_args(self) -> list[str]:
         import warnings
@@ -465,6 +488,55 @@ class Context:
             "auto_envvar_prefix": self.auto_envvar_prefix,
         }
 
+    def save_state(self) -> dict[str, t.Any]:
+        """Capture a snapshot of this context's current parameters,
+        options, and user/meta state so it can later be reverted with
+        :meth:`restore_state`.
+
+        Mutable attributes that could otherwise leak changes made
+        after the snapshot (such as ``params`` or ``obj``) are deep
+        copied so that later in-place mutations don't affect the
+        saved snapshot.
+
+        .. versionadded:: 8.2
+        """
+        import copy
+
+        return {
+            "params": copy.deepcopy(self.params),
+            "args": list(self.args),
+            "_protected_args": list(self._protected_args),
+            "_opt_prefixes": set(self._opt_prefixes),
+            "obj": copy.deepcopy(self.obj),
+            "_meta": copy.deepcopy(self._meta),
+            "_parameter_source": copy.deepcopy(self._parameter_source),
+            "default_map": copy.deepcopy(self.default_map),
+            "info_name": self.info_name,
+            "invoked_subcommand": self.invoked_subcommand,
+            "terminal_width": self.terminal_width,
+            "max_content_width": self.max_content_width,
+            "allow_extra_args": self.allow_extra_args,
+            "allow_interspersed_args": self.allow_interspersed_args,
+            "ignore_unknown_options": self.ignore_unknown_options,
+            "help_option_names": list(self.help_option_names),
+            "token_normalize_func": self.token_normalize_func,
+            "resilient_parsing": self.resilient_parsing,
+            "auto_envvar_prefix": self.auto_envvar_prefix,
+            "color": self.color,
+            "show_default": self.show_default,
+        }
+
+    def restore_state(self, state: dict[str, t.Any]) -> None:
+        """Revert this context's attributes back to a snapshot
+        previously captured with :meth:`save_state`.
+
+        :param state: The dictionary returned by :meth:`save_state`.
+
+        .. versionadded:: 8.2
+        """
+        for key, value in state.items():
+            setattr(self, key, value)
+
     def __enter__(self) -> Context:
         self._depth += 1
         push_context(self)
@@ -606,11 +678,32 @@ class Context:
         """Invoke all close callbacks registered with
         :meth:`call_on_close`, and exit all context managers entered
         with :meth:`with_resource`.
+
+        :raises RuntimeError: if :attr:`validate_nesting` is enabled
+            and this context still has open child contexts.
+
+        .. versionchanged:: 8.2
+            Raises a ``RuntimeError`` if :attr:`validate_nesting` is
+            enabled and child contexts are still open.
         """
+        if self.validate_nesting and self._children:
+            names = ", ".join(repr(child.info_name) for child in self._children)
+            raise RuntimeError(
+
